steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student claims opening weekend earnings can reliably predict Oscar recognition. To weaken that claim, we need a case where higher opening weekend earnings fail to produce Oscar nomination while lower earnings do.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The Girl Who Played with Fire vs. Crouching Tiger': The Girl Who Played with Fire had higher opening weekend earnings (~$7.5M) than Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon (~$5.9M), yet was not nominated while Crouching Tiger was. This directly contradicts the prediction. ✓
  • Choice B — 'lifetime earnings comparison': compares total earnings, not opening weekend earnings, so it does not address the student's claim. ✗
  • Choice C — 'inaccurate data': the figures cited do not match the graph, making this choice unreliable as evidence. ✗
  • Choice D — 'irrelevant factors': addresses a metric unrelated to opening weekend performance. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
